Now before we dive into this module, let me give you an example of the power of Scripps that I think will really bring this point home for you. Now I assume you've seen the movie Braveheart with Mel Gibson, you probably have. Well there's a scene towards the middle where he rides into his horse in front of the troops with a blue paint on his face and they're about to go to battle and they're heavily outnumbered by the English. So he gives this unbelievably rousing this famous speech where he says, you know, Sons of Scotland, I stand before you today in the opposition of 300 years of rape and tyranny and the murder of our sons and daughters and blah blah blah blah blah right? They may take our lives but they'll never take our freedom and it's an amazing speech and he raises his hand, he screams and the troops scream and they have charging ahead and they wipe out the superior English troops, right? You all remember that speech, it was pretty incredible, right? Well let me ask you a question. Do you think old Mel winged it? I mean do you think the producer said to Mel, you know, hey Mel, here's what you're gonna do, you're gonna dress up in this great outfit with the blue paint on your face, you're gonna ride in the horse with all these people staying there and I want you to just kind of give it a whirl and try to motivate them. So they wouldn't leave it up to chance that maybe Mel Gibson will say the best possible thing on screen. They take that out of the equation by hiring the best screenwriters in the world who polish the dialogue again and again and again until it sounds exactly completely natural in every way and makes sense to the audience, not only logically but it moves them emotionally as well and that's exactly what great movies like brave bottle of forest gum do. They move us both logically and emotionally, essentially closing us on loving the movie. Now another thing it's important to understand is that the use of scripts is just as crucial in face-to-face sales as with selling over the telephone. Now obviously you can't be holding a script in your hand while you're staying in front of someone so I'm gonna show you how to memorize some simple yet very powerful language patterns that are specific to each industry and then show you how to hook them together to create perfect scripts on the fly which is an absolutely essential part of being a first-class straight line salesperson. You need to have first-class scripts all the way. Here's what we're gonna do. First I'm gonna walk you through step-by-step how to prepare an amazing script, how to write one, how to tweak it until it sounds perfect and then show you how to practice by yourself or role playing with someone else which is even better until you sound absolutely perfect. Now before I get into the specifics here let me first go through the behavioral competency I've chosen for this module and it's called strategic preparation and what strategic preparation is it's the attitude that almost borders on over preparation for a sale starting with the line-by-line scripting of every possible client interaction you can think of. In simple terms it means anticipating everything that might possibly come up in a sale with a prospect and having the perfect response prepared in advance. Now obviously to pull this off you have to do a bit of homework here right you gotta essentially research your client's needs their background as much as possible and you have to imagine yourself literally walking in your client's shoes so you can understand the specific pain points their wants and desires and their triggers so you can then write scripts that will solve all of these issues that your clients are facing that's strategic preparation make sense. What happens is when I get into a situation of influence I prepare myself to the point where I'm so at ease with the information that I know what I'm going to say before I say it so if I if I'm at that point just imagine this what happens is guess what the processing power that would normally be tied up having to think about what I'm saying now I'm actually reciting a script to you now it doesn't sound like I'm reciting a script it sounds completely natural right the same way an actor who memorizes a script you know it doesn't seem like there it's canned if it's good acting and good writing what happens it just seems perfectly natural on on stage on camera you're watching on TV or in the movies so what happens is if I take a person who is not that good at juggling various thoughts but I get them to memorize the core language patterns the core points they need to get across to someone else then all of a sudden they know those by heart so when they're saying those there is no processing just like repeating it so now they still have some power to think ahead and plan ahead for what's going on right so the key is is the the aunt and I like I think they just say well some people have this great quality and others don't true but the place to go with that is okay and if you don't have then what do you do to offset that and when you offset that is to treat your preparation and free your conscious mind up by having stuff you know so well it requires no processing power
